full_text:
Tribus meeting 
30.03.2023

Introduction 
‚óèRecent contributions from multiplexed imaging 
‚óèPotential shortcomings on those contributions due to cell type calling limitations 
‚óèCell type calling as a bottleneck to gain full potential from new technologies 
‚óèRecent contributions from spatial analysis to tumor microenvironment <- why it matters 
‚óèWe address this gap by designing an algorithm that does X Y and Z. 
‚óèWe validate our contribution with this and that metric 
‚óèMore accurate/clean cell type labels enable this and that fun/relevant/important results 
(here quick spatial analysis with scimap).

Results I. 
Tribus yields rapid and accurate cell type calling in large datasets 
‚óèShow how Tribus agrees with pathologist calls, to evaluate the quality given ground 
truth. 
‚óãmetrics for clustering using the ground truth labels 
‚óèMaybe TMA public data, data from STELLAR paper? 
‚óèshow the runtime on different sizes of datasets 
‚óèshow difference between single-cell annotation and batch annotation

Results II. 
Comparison using clustering metrics 
‚óècomparison based on qualitative traits (runtime, user interaction, need for prior 
knowledge, usability, visual exploration/interactivity) 
‚óèunsupervised clustering metrics 
‚óèmethods to compare with: 
‚óãmanual gating 
‚óãflowSOM 
‚óãlook for other tools for similar purpose

Questions for results 
‚óèhttps://docs.google.com/document/d/16owdxRrrYUpR-mDFzwyNbKSJ6Kgnm
NeDanJxXWg3dMY/edit# 
‚óèDo we want spatial analysis in the manuscript? 
‚óãit could show the power of a rapid and reproducible cell type calling 
‚óãnot really part of TRIBUS (in Julia‚Äôs manuscript draft it is mentioned) 
‚óèSomething else? 
‚óãfurther analysis and comparisons?

(logic)

Methods - TRIBUS II. 
Outputs of TRIBUS 
‚óèLabels table 
‚óãlabel for each level (None values) 
‚óãfinal label based on the last level 
‚óãcontaining ‚Äúother_level‚Äù, ‚Äúundefined_level‚Äù 
‚óèScore table 
‚óãscore for each level 
‚óãfinal score based on last level

‚óãheatmap for median expression values per 
cell_types

cell_types 
‚óãevery level? 
‚óãmore?

Methods - TRIBUS III. 
If not enough cell ‚Üí cell by cell scoring 
Clustering 
‚óèSOM: fast, tried out others it worked the best (do we have to show this in the 
manuscript?) 
‚óèparameter of SOM: grid_size = sqrt(data_size)*5 (by V esanto )
‚óèCalculating median expression of markers for each cluster 
Scoring 
‚óèEach cluster gets a score for each cell type 
‚óèMean squared error from 
‚óãMaximum value (99th percentile) for positive markers of a cell type 
‚óãMinimum value for negative markers of a cell type 
‚óãSquared errors are 0-1 normalised before mean calculation

Methods - Framework I. 
-as a pip package 
-available from terminal and python module too
-Terminal :
-input and output paths, how many levels, plots (if we want or not) 
-Library 
-it returns the result tables 
-also visualization functions available 
-example Notebook 
-Napari plugin as a pip package

Methods - Napari 
-Where to put it? 
-Should it get its own section? (Or go into the framework implementation 
entirely) 
-How detailed the methods/ideas should be? (Eg. discussing .zarr) ‚Üí My idea 
would be discussing it in a small section (what does it add for Tribus, what 
user can do), but not going into too deep details ‚Üí Not novel technique

Potential test datasets I. 
Stellar 
‚óèIntestine, tonsil, Barett‚Äôs Esophagus (BE) tissue 
‚óèAnnotated by experts 
‚óèCODEX multiplexed imaging, z-score normalised data 
‚óèSmall intestine: 137652 cells, 4 regions (44170, 30067, 26494, 36921 cells 
respectively) 
‚óèColon: 110633 cells, 4 regions (33793, 21232, 30545, 25063 cells respectively) 
‚óèTonsil: 173968 cells 
‚óèBE: 45958 cells (annotated by STELLAR) 
‚óèWe need to think of an adequate celltype description table

Potential datasets II. - In-house data 
‚óèSciset 
‚óãHGSOC tissue sample 
‚óã460360 cells 
‚óãContains labels and celltype description table 
‚óãLabels were assigned by first version of Tribus and manual gating (Inga-Maria) 
‚óãLog2 transformation 
‚óèNKI-TMA-18-810 
‚óã765196 cells 
‚óã140 cores, but cellIDs do not overlap (continuous from core to core) 
‚óãIndividual core cell number ranges between 1208 and 11087 
‚óèTMA 
‚óã3 patients, cores need to be separated for avoiding cellID duplicates, or re-indexing for bigger 
cohort 
‚óã2307, 5766, 2306 cells per patient (patient number 53768, 55320, 60765) 
‚óãPotentially good set for the single cell annotation feature
